text.title: Agentic Market Sizing Patterns
text.summary: Orchestrator prompt, CLI/SaaS table, gotchas, and best practices for AI-driven market sizing pipelines.

section.title: Orchestrator Pattern

rule.statement: A Claude Sonnet agent handles the full sizing loop: receive market definition, generate separate top-down and bottom-up queries, dispatch to Perplexity or web-search, collect results, output structured estimate with confidence ranges and assumption log.
rule.rationale: The structured loop prevents the most common failure: mixing data from both paths before triangulation. Explicit instruction to keep paths independent is required in the system prompt, not assumed.

example.code: <market_sizing_task> <market>Cloud-based AI research tools for knowledge workers, global, 2026</market> <approach>Run top-down and bottom-up independently. Do not share intermediate results between paths.</approach> <top_down>Find total knowledge-worker software market → isolate AI tools segment → apply addressable fraction</top_down> <bottom_up>Estimate target companies × seats × annual price</bottom_up> <output> - TAM, SAM, SOM with confidence range (low/mid/high) - Assumption log (one line per assumption) - Source list with access date </output> </market_sizing_task>
example.note: Explicitly separates the two paths and requires assumption logging. Human reviews assumption log before estimate enters any deliverable.

section.reference: Claude SDK triangulator that runs two paths and flags divergence >2x
Full XML prompt template for market sizing tasks

list.item: pytrends — Google Trends demand signal proxy (pip install pytrends)
serpapi — Structured search for market reports (pip install google-search-results)
pandas — Tabulate and triangulate estimates (pip install pandas)
anthropic SDK — Drive Claude for synthesis and calculation (pip install anthropic)
Statista — export only, agent must parse downloaded PDFs
CB Insights — CSV export, company counts, funding data for bottom-up
Perplexity Pro — REST API, best for synthesizing analyst commentary
AlphaSense — partial API, strong for public-company disclosures
Exploding Topics — REST API, demand signal for emerging markets
Google Trends — free, pytrends, proxy for consumer demand (not reliable for B2B)

antipattern.description: Agents conflate TAM and SAM without explicit definitions in the system prompt.
antipattern.reason: Both definitions must be injected in the system prompt on every run; they are not preserved across sessions.

antipattern.description: LLMs anchor heavily on the first data point encountered; collecting all sources before synthesizing is not the default behavior.
antipattern.reason: Anchoring bias from early data inflates confidence; instruct the agent to collect all sources before producing any number.

antipattern.description: Models omit confidence ranges unless explicitly required in the output schema.
antipattern.reason: Point estimates invite false precision; confidence ranges must be a required field in the output schema, not an optional addition.

antipattern.description: Statista and CB Insights data lags 6-18 months; AI-synthesized estimates inherit that lag without disclosure.
antipattern.reason: Source recency must be verified by the agent and reported alongside the estimate; stale sources must be flagged explicitly.
